task.title: Plan Sessions for the Week
task.shortdesc: Allocate your weekly time budget across active projects by creating and scheduling sessions at the start of each week.

context.p: Weekly session planning translates your portfolio priorities into concrete time blocks. Do this at the start of each week—before the week's demands pull your attention elsewhere.

step.stepresult: The Dashboard shows each active project's score from last week. Projects showing Red or Yellow status need session attention first.

step.cmd: Click the Sessions tab and confirm the week selector shows the current week.
Check the weekly budget bar at the bottom of the Sessions tab.

info.p: Note how much time is available. This is your planning constraint.

step.cmd: Allocate sessions to red and yellow projects first.

info.p: Projects that stalled last week need at least one session this week to recover. Create those sessions before allocating time to green projects.

step.cmd: Create or promote sessions for each active project until the weekly budget bar reflects your intended allocation.

ul.li: The project you named as primary focus in your weekly review deserves at least two sessions.
Every active project deserves at least one session per week. If you cannot schedule a session for a project, consider moving it to Backlog temporarily.
Do not over-schedule. If the planned time exceeds your budget, remove lower-priority sessions or move them to a future week.

step.cmd: Set all committed sessions to Planned status.
step.stepresult: The budget bar shows your total planned hours for the week.

result.p: Your week is planned. Each session represents a concrete commitment to a project. Return to the Sessions tab after each session to mark it Done.
